L. 111–148, title V, § 5103(b)124 Stat. 605Mar. 23, 2010“Not later than 180 days after the date of enactment of this Act [], the responsibilities and resources of the National Center for Health Workforce Analysis, as in effect on the date before the date of enactment of this Act, shall be transferred to the National Center for Health Care Workforce Analysis established under section 761 of the Public Health Service Act [], as amended by subsection (a).”Mar. 23, 201042 U.S.C. 294n

children.text: provide for the development of information describing the health professions workforce and the analysis of workforce related issues; and
provide necessary information for decision-making regarding future directions in health professions and nursing programs in response to societal and professional needs.

children.heading: National Center for Health Care Workforce Analysis
children.label: b National Center for Health Care Workforce Analysis

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish the National Center for Health Workforce Analysis (referred to in this section as the “National Center”).

children.label: A
children.text: provide for the development of information describing and analyzing the health care workforce and workforce related issues;

children.label: B
children.text: carry out the activities under ;section 295k(a) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: annually evaluate programs under this subchapter;

children.label: D
children.text: develop and publish performance measures and benchmarks for programs under this subchapter; and

children.label: E
children.text: establish, maintain, and publicize a national Internet registry of each grant awarded under this subchapter and a database to collect data from longitudinal evaluations (as described in subsection (d)(2)) on performance measures (as developed under sections 293(d)(3), 294f(d)(3), and 294(a)(3) of this title).lo

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The National Center shall collaborate with Federal agencies and relevant professional and educational organizations or societies for the purpose of linking data regarding grants awarded under this subchapter.

children.heading: Contracts for health workforce analysis
children.label: B Contracts for health workforce analysis
children.text: For the purpose of carrying out the activities described in subparagraph (A), the National Center may enter into contracts with relevant professional and educational organizations or societies.

children.heading: State and regional Centers for Health Workforce Analysis
children.label: c State and regional Centers for Health Workforce Analysis
A
children.text: collecting, analyzing, and reporting data regarding programs under this subchapter to the National Center and to the public; and

children.label: B
children.text: providing technical assistance to local and regional entities on the collection, analysis, and reporting of data.

children.label: A
children.text: be a State, a State workforce investment board, a public health or health professions school, an academic health center, or an appropriate public or private nonprofit entity; and

children.label: B
children.text: submit to the Secretary an application at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Increase in grants for longitudinal evaluations
children.label: d Increase in grants for longitudinal evaluations

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall increase the amount awarded to an eligible entity under this subchapter for a longitudinal evaluation of individuals who have received education, training, or financial assistance from programs under this subchapter.

children.label: A
children.text: studying practice patterns; and

children.label: B
children.text: collecting and reporting data on performance measures developed under sections 293(d)(3), 294f(d)(3), and 294(a)(3) of this title.lo

children.heading: Guidelines
children.label: 3 Guidelines
children.text: A longitudinal evaluation shall comply with guidelines issued under sections 293(d)(4), 294f(d)(4), and 294(a)(4) of this title.lo

children.heading: Eligible entities
children.label: 4 Eligible entities
children.text: To be eligible to obtain an increase under this section, an entity shall be a recipient of a grant or contract under this subchapter.

children.heading: National Center
children.label: A National Center
children.text: To carry out subsection (b), there are authorized to be appropriated $5,663,000 for each of fiscal years 2021 through 2025.

children.heading: State and regional Centers
children.label: B State and regional Centers
children.text: To carry out subsection (c), there are authorized to be appropriated $4,500,000 for each of fiscal years 2010 through 2014.

children.heading: Grants for longitudinal evaluations
children.label: C Grants for longitudinal evaluations
children.text: To carry out subsection (d), there are authorized to be appropriated such sums as may be necessary for fiscal years 2010 through 2014.

children.heading: Reservation
children.label: 2 Reservation
children.text: Of the amounts appropriated under paragraph (1) for a fiscal year, the Secretary shall reserve not less than $600,000 for conducting health professions research and for carrying out data collection and analysis in accordance with .section 295k of this title

children.heading: Availability of additional funds
children.label: 3 Availability of additional funds
children.text: Amounts otherwise appropriated for programs or activities under this subchapter may be used for activities under subsection (b) with respect to the programs or activities from which such amounts were made available.
